timeline.label: Q1 FY2026: net sales $23.1B (+10.3% YoY); comparable sales +0.6%; gross margin compressed to 32.7%
FY2025: FBM and ADG acquisitions drive Other segment to $2.2B sales; share repurchases paused to $211M; long-term debt rose to $37.5B
Updated Total Home strategy aligned to home improvement demand drivers; Pro customer remains core focus
FY2024: net sales $83.7B (-3.0% YoY); comparable sales -0.5%; repurchased $4.1B of stock
FY2023: net sales $86.4B (-10.9% YoY post-COVID normalization); comparable sales -3.5%
FY2022: net sales $97.1B peak; comparable sales +0.3%; pandemic-era DIY demand elevated
FY2021: net sales $96.3B (+33.6% YoY); pandemic DIY boom; repurchased $14.1B of stock
Total Home strategy launched: Pro customer, online acceleration, installation services, localization
Marvin R. Ellison became President and CEO (from J.C. Penney); led turnaround and Pro focus
Lowe's went public on NYSE; founded 1921 in North Wilkesboro, NC

industry.structure: Highly fragmented U.S. home improvement market dominated by a duopoly: Lowe's (~1,759 stores) and Home Depot compete on location, assortment, Pro services, and fulfillment. Wholesale distribution (FBM, ADG) adds a B2B layer.
industry.share_shift: Lowe's gained Pro and online share under Total Home but trailed Home Depot on comps in recent years; FY2025 acquisitions of FBM and ADG expand wholesale/building-products reach.
industry.trend: Post-rate-hike housing slowdown pressured transaction counts (-2.8% FY2025) while average ticket rose (+3.0%) on inflation and mix; Pro and installation services are the strategic growth vectors.
